text: Deep learning has transformed many applications of computer vision and natural language processing with the help of transfer learning. They train a base model on a large number of images or texts (pretraining), and then fine-tune it with a smaller number of data for a specific application. In network biology, it is common that the transcriptional regulatory networks (TRNs) of genes remain undetermined in rare diseases or for inaccessible or scarce tissues. Here, we propose Geneformer, a context-aware and attention-based deep learning model that was pre-trained on a large-scale dataset of 30 million single-cell transcriptomes. The Geneformer self-supervises learning universal regulatory patterns in gene regulation and extracts from the attention weights the encoding of pairwise relationships within the single-cell expression profiles. We assess the downstream performances of Geneformer in a variety of applications on small task-specific datasets, which are restricted in number of samples, low number of genes and short lengths. In each cases, Geneformer led to an improved performance. In addition, we predicted potential genes of interest in cardiomyopathy using in silico perturbations, and the potential target genes predicted by our model improved the contractile function of cardiomyocytes derived from an iPSC disease model.
